novel_features.name: Road-trip corridor
novel_features.description: Find Atlas Obscura wonders along the driving corridor between two cities, not just in one place.
novel_features.rationale: Requires geocoding both endpoints, sampling the path, near-searching each point, and deduping into one corridor view that no live single-place scraper produces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user is driving between two places and wants worthwhile stops along the way.

novel_features.name: Saved trips
novel_features.description: Accumulate places into named itineraries that persist across sessions.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a durable local SQLite store; live scrapers have no memory between invocations.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to build up a trip over multiple sessions instead of re-searching each time.

novel_features.name: Visited tracking
novel_features.description: Record which wonders you've seen, with optional date and note.
novel_features.rationale: Session-spanning personal state only possible with a persistent local store.
novel_features.example: atlas-obscura-pp-cli visited mark salvation-mountain --note "worth the desert drive"
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to remember what you've already seen so gaps and surprise can skip them.

novel_features.name: Unvisited gaps
novel_features.description: Show good wonders near a point that you haven't visited yet, ranked by interestingness.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the cached places against the local visited table offline; a live scraper has no visited state to diff against.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to plan what's left to see near a place you're revisiting.

novel_features.name: Walkable clusters
novel_features.description: Group nearby wonders into spatially tight clusters that make a walkable half-day.
novel_features.rationale: Clustering over a full cached result set is instant locally; a live scraper would need many paginated radius calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to turn a pile of nearby wonders into an efficient day on foot.

novel_features.name: Daily surprise
novel_features.description: Pick one high-interest wonder you haven't visited, seeded by date so it's stable per day.
novel_features.rationale: Date-stable selection that excludes visited places requires a persistent corpus and a local visited join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in a daily agent heartbeat to surface a fresh wonder without repeats.

novel_features.name: Export trip
novel_features.description: Serialize a saved trip to GPX, GeoJSON, or a markdown itinerary, fully offline.
novel_features.rationale: Pulls coords, descriptions, and Know-Before-You-Go from the local cache with zero network calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to hand a road trip to a GPS, a map tool, or a human-readable log.

narrative.headline: Every Atlas Obscura search, plus a local database, road-trip corridor routing, and saved trips no other Atlas Obscura tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Search the world's hidden wonders by keyword or coordinates, pull full place detail with Know-Before-You-Go notes, and mirror it all into a local SQLite store for offline, agent-native use. Then go further than any scraper: find wonders along a driving route, save and export trips, and track what you've visited. Community-sourced from atlasobscura.com — not an official API.

quickstart.comment: health check; works with no setup since Atlas Obscura needs no API key
wonders within 5 miles of a place, sorted by distance
full detail incl. Know Before You Go

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for a place name
troubleshoots.fix: Place geocoding uses Open-Meteo; try a more specific name ("Portland, Oregon") or pass explicit lat,lng to near.
Category is filtered client-side from place tags; raise --max-scan-pages or --radius to widen the scan.
Pass --refresh on show/near to bypass the local cache TTL and re-fetch from atlasobscura.com.
The CLI keeps a low request rate by default; slow down with --limit and rely on the local cache for repeat queries.

narrative.when_to_use: Use Atlas Obscura when a user wants offbeat, unusual, or hidden points of interest — for trip planning, road trips, or adding curiosity stops to an itinerary. It excels at corridor routing between two cities, accumulating saved trips, and offline re-querying of a local mirror. Reach for it when the task is discovery of weird/wonderful places rather than mainstream travel booking.
anti_triggers: Do not use for booking hotels, flights, or restaurants — it only surfaces places.
Do not use for mainstream tourist attractions or reviews; it is curated oddities, not a general POI directory.
Do not use for real-time opening hours or ticketing — Atlas Obscura has no structured hours; check the place's own site.

recipes.title: Plan stops along a road trip
recipes.explanation: Surfaces the best-scoring wonders within the driving corridor between two cities.

recipes.title: Lean nearby scan for an agent
recipes.explanation: Deeply nested response narrowed to just title, distance, and URL so an agent doesn't parse image and coordinate noise.

recipes.title: Build and export a trip
recipes.command: atlas-obscura-pp-cli trip add winchester-mystery-house --trip ca && atlas-obscura-pp-cli export ca --format md
recipes.explanation: Accumulate places into a named trip, then render a markdown itinerary with descriptions and Know Before You Go.

recipes.title: What haven't I seen near home
recipes.explanation: Cross-references cached wonders against your visited log and returns only the worthwhile unseen ones.
